_intent: Single source of truth for every tunable in the E:/hooks system. Scripts can read shared + their own entry; Stop completion is wired via stop-completion-chain.mjs in Codex + Claude Code.

shared._projectsNote: ONE model replacing divergent hardcoded project lists. Derive: detection slugs = slug + aliases; recall scope = matched-slug + 'all'; rebuild set (= tag-normalizer ACTIVE, gov-gate scope) = kind=='rebuild'; PROJECT set = all slugs + 'all'. Skill indexing is intentionally separate: E:/_skills warehouse + skills-catalog.md allowlist only.
shared.stopwords: a an the and or but if then else for to of in on at by with from into over under is are was were be been being do does did done have has had will would can could should may might must this that these those it its as we you they he she them our your their what when where why how which who whom not no yes ok okay today now here there about out up down off so just like get got need want try also use using used make made making go going gone work working please thanks let lets sure really very more most one two new add added update updated change changed thing things stuff way ways back next first last

taskPolicy._doc: Active Task Envelope + Stop policy tunables. See _briefs/2026-06-21-stop-task-policy-control-plane-implementation-plan.md. Phrase/class patterns default in task-policy/task-policy-core.mjs unless overridden here.

hooks.name: Protocol + Recall Injector
hooks.description: Push the A-E protocol + ranked memory recall + curated skill suggestion into every prompt. Fail-open.

settings._model: Three stages: terms (parse the prompt) -> sources (compose injected content from protocol + skills + memory) -> output (cap + labels).

terms._note: Terms are extracted search words from the current prompt plus recent user prompts. minLen is the shortest token kept. max is the maximum extracted terms passed into memory and skill search. contextPrompts is how many earlier user prompts are included.

runtime._note: Inject-protocol runtime guardrails. Keep subprocess buffers, transcript-tail reads, and diagnostic clipping here so code does not hide operational tunables.

protocol._note: Static A-E rules text. Injected verbatim as the BASE of every output; memory + skills are appended.

memory._note: Memory recall funnel: SQLite/vector is primary until Hindsight is backfilled from E:/_memory/memory-sqlite.db. Hindsight config stays wired for a future cutover; fallback is off until then.

scoring._note: Memory reranking: FTS match strength, recency, and confidence are converted to 0..100 scores, combined by weight, then filtered by relativeFloor before final output.

skills._note: Skill suggestion funnel: candidatePool is raw skill hits to fetch before reranking. max is final suggested skills injected into the prompt.

scoring._note: Skill reranking: FTS favors name/description/content by fieldBoosts; overlap checks how many extracted terms appear in the skill text.

overlap._note: minTerms here is different from memory.minTerms. It means a skill candidate must overlap with this many extracted terms to survive skill filtering.

skills._noiseTermsNote: Terms in this list are ignored for skill search because they are too common or too vague to identify the right skill.

output._note: capChars is the maximum hidden additional context emitted by this hook. budgets cap each section before the whole output cap is applied.

hooks.name: Hook Telemetry Logger
hooks.description: PostToolUse + PostToolUseFailure logger: append every tool firing to hook_events in the dedicated hooks DB (shared.paths.hooksDb). Status is event-derived (PostToolUseFailure=error; PostToolUse=ok unless the response envelope structurally signals an error) — no free-text scan. Observability substrate for loop-safety. Honors enabled/failPolicy/match via _core/hook_runtime.py. Fail-open, never blocks.

hooks.name: Sequential Thinking Gate
hooks.description: PreToolUse gate that requires an unexpired same-session successful sequential-thinking MCP call with remaining grant uses. The configured thinking tools are exempt so the gate cannot deadlock the required action.

settings._browserNote: browser_* tools and CallMcpTool→cursor-ide-browser are also exempt in thinking-gate.py (live localhost verification must not consume planning grants).
settings._mode: Mutating tools consume bounded grant. readOnlyTools are exempt and do not consume grant uses.

hooks.name: Task Mode Classifier
hooks.description: UserPromptSubmit: classify task mode (explore/implement/fix/refactor/review/docs) and inject required planning skills. Writes session state for planning-start-gate.

hooks.name: Planning Start Gate
hooks.description: PreToolUse: before mutating tools, require mode-appropriate skill checkpoint (or sequential-thinking) for implement/fix/refactor/review sessions.

hooks.name: Task Policy Guard
hooks.description: PreToolUse: enforce the Active Task Envelope's explicit restrictions (read-only mutations, forbidden scopes, and heavy browser/full-suite commands). Denies only what the active task explicitly forbids; fail-open for ordinary read-only tools.

hooks.name: Stop Memory Harvester
hooks.description: Stop hook that scans the recent session transcript and writes durable facts into SQLite memory. Stop fires after every assistant response, but runAfterNewExchanges controls extraction cadence and reviewLastExchanges controls the review window. Default extraction uses a local Codex subscription proxy (no Platform API key). Fail-open; never blocks completion.

vectorStore._doc: Store harvester rows through mcp-memory-service sqlite_vec so each memory has an embedding. Hindsight remains disabled until backfill/cutover is ready.

existingMemoryContext._doc: Before Spark decides what to retain, retrieve related active SQLite memories so it can emit insert/skip/supersede operations instead of blindly appending rows.

extraction._doc: mode=llm sends one prompt to the local Codex proxy; mode=heuristic uses keyword rules. On LLM failure, fallbackMode controls whether heuristic runs.

llm._doc: OpenAI-compatible local proxy using Codex subscription OAuth (~/.codex/auth.json). Keep running via E:/hooks/codex-proxy/install-startup-task.ps1 (task JWC-Codex-Proxy-8787).
llm.systemPrompt: You extract durable memory operations from agent session transcripts. Return ONLY a JSON array. Each item must be an object with "operation" (insert|skip|supersede), "content" (string, empty only for skip), optional "memory_type" (decision|mistake|reference|learning|planning), optional "confidence" (0..1), optional "reason", optional "evidence", and optional "supersedes_id" for supersede. Skip greetings, acknowledgements, one-off task chatter, and facts already represented by existing memories. No markdown fences or prose outside the JSON array.
llm.existingMemoryHeader: Existing active memories that may already cover or conflict with the transcript:
llm.taskPrompt: From the exchanges above, decide memory operations worth applying. Use insert for genuinely new durable facts. Use skip with a reason for garbage, generic chatter, or facts already represented. Use supersede when the transcript corrects or replaces an existing active memory; set supersedes_id to the existing memory id. Prefer explicit Decision/Why/Wrong/Right phrasing when present.

heuristic._doc: Keyword fallback when extraction.mode=heuristic or LLM fails and fallbackMode=heuristic.

hindsight._doc: After SQLite store, copy the same memory text to Hindsight with strategy=exact (no second LLM). Spark already extracted on the SQLite path. DISABLED until backfill is ready.

retainLlm._doc: Hindsight retain LLM - same Codex Spark proxy contract as memory-harvester settings.extraction.llm

settings._note: Runs first inside stop-completion-chain. Stores through the sqlite_vec bridge so memory rows and vector embeddings are created together; Hindsight remains secondary/off.

hooks.name: Memory Mutation Normalizer
hooks.description: Legacy SQLite/vector memory PostToolUse observer. Finds affected SQLite memory rows, applies the retain normalizer to tags/type/metadata, and records update/skip/audit/fail visibility in hook_events. Hindsight sync_retain is the durable memory direction; this hook does not mutate Hindsight. Fail-open; never rewrites memory content, FTS rows, or vector rows.

hooks.name: Loop-Safety Retry Breaker
hooks.description: PreToolUse circuit-breaker. Reads the hook_events telemetry substrate (shared.paths.hooksDb) to count consecutive same-error failures of the SAME operation; soft-injects a change-approach directive at softMax and DENIES the tool call at hardMax. No external state store. Honors enabled/match/failPolicy via _core/hook_runtime.py. Fail-open: a guard error never blocks a tool.

settings._thresholds: Consecutive same-operation, same-error failures. softMax=inject change-approach directive (tool still allowed). hardMax=deny the tool call. Derived from references/agentihooks retry_breaker dual-threshold; reimplemented native against SQLite hook_events (reset-on-success via walk-until-first-success scan).
settings._outputContract: Cross-runtime PreToolUse JSON, verified against both vendor hook docs. HARD: hookSpecificOutput{hookEventName:PreToolUse, permissionDecision:deny, permissionDecisionReason}. SOFT: hookSpecificOutput{hookEventName:PreToolUse, additionalContext} + top-level systemMessage. Claude reads additionalContext; Codex reads systemMessage. Always exits 0; decision rides in JSON.
settings._matchNote: Codex reports apply_patch edits as tool_name 'apply_patch'; Claude reports Edit/Write/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit. Both listed so the breaker covers edits on both runtimes.

hooks.name: Quality Completion Gate
hooks.description: Phase executor used by completion-quality-gate. Computes changed domains, maps changed files through a verify manifest, runs manifest commands, and gates on exit codes.

hooks.name: Prohibited Fraud Completion Gate
hooks.description: Stop hook that enforces the canonical prohibited fraudulent implementation methods document across JWC, KC, TFO, kai-agent, and DBASE, then scans current implementation diffs for prohibited source-of-truth, fake-state, fallback, and verification patterns.

requiredPhrases: Browser storage for any of these
if it scopes, authorizes, routes, mutates, verifies, persists, displays durable product state, or proves runtime behavior
settings._note: This is the canonical Stop hook for Jon's prohibited fraudulent implementation methods requirement. It checks document presence/content for all five repos and current changed lines in the active target repo.

hooks.name: Agent Diff Completion Gate
hooks.description: Phase executor used by completion-quality-gate for configured repo diff policies: matching changed files trip files-or-LOC thresholds, then live verification -> verification-before-completion; large LOC adds waza-hunt.

settings._note: Repo policies are config-driven. Each enabled repo has rules[] with path matchers and files-or-loc trigger thresholds. Large LOC diffs add waza-hunt and up to 3 remediation loops.

hooks.name: Completion Quality Gate
hooks.description: Merged Stop hook for required quality checks and implementation-risk/live-verification policy. Replaces separate quality-completion-gate and agent-diff-completion-gate Stop-chain entries while preserving those executors as internal phases.

settings._note: This is the only Stop-chain quality/risk gate. It passes the same task-policy changed-file list to the quality phase and risk/live-verification phase, then emits one completion decision.

hooks.name: Frontend Design Gate
hooks.description: Stop gate: block completion when the current change ADDS raw HTML primitives (<button>/<input>/<select>/<textarea>) in app code where the repo design system already provides a components/ui/<name>.tsx component. Changed-lines only (git diff vs HEAD + untracked), never the existing backlog. Reads git, not telemetry. Loop-safe (maxRepeatedBlocks) and fail-open.

settings._note: Per-repo design-system inventory is derived at runtime from each repo's components/ui/*.tsx; a primitive is enforced only when its component exists. Changed-lines regex with string/comment stripping (v1; AST later). enabled=false until wired into settings.json Stop + validated.

scripts.name: Config Schema Generator
scripts.description: Generates config.schema.json from _core/config-model.mjs. The schema is derived output, not the hand-maintained source of truth.

scripts.name: Stop Completion Chain
scripts.description: Canonical Stop orchestrator for Codex and Claude Code. Runs enabled completion gates in order: prohibited-fraud -> completion-quality.

settings._wireDoc: memory-harvester runs as a fail-open pre-step before blocking gates; prohibited-fraud and completion-quality are blocking completion gates.

scripts.name: Runtime Hooks Validator
scripts.description: Validates config.json against _core/config-model.mjs, checks that config.schema.json is generated from that model, and verifies enabled script paths.

scripts.name: Hook Development Test Runner
scripts.description: Config-aware hook development utility borrowed from hook-development patterns. Generates sample event payloads, lints hook config/script contracts, and only executes a selected hook when --hook is explicitly provided.

scripts.name: Skill Indexer
scripts.description: Rebuilds skills/skills_fts in the memory DB from on-disk SKILL.md files. Derived mirror — full rebuild, no stale rows. Run manually after adding/editing/removing skills.

scripts.name: Memory Tag Normalizer
scripts.description: Enforces the locked project-tag taxonomy on the memory store (tags column only; safe). Dry-run by default; --apply writes. Reports untagged entries, never guesses.

settings._activeSlugsNote: = shared.projects where kind=='rebuild'. Listed for the python script; keep in sync with shared.projects.

_notWired.reason: Stop completion is wired through stop-completion-chain.mjs. Cursor may use per-gate entries via adapters/cursor/run-hook.mjs. Runtime wiring is controlled by this config.
cutoverRequires: Trust updated Stop hook in ~/.codex/config.toml after editing
Restart Claude Code after adding Stop hook to ~/.claude/settings.json
